body: Are you ready to elevate your baseball skills? Mark your calendars for Monday, July 14th! The FTB Falcons are hosting an exhilarating PBR showcase in Brooklyn, NY. This event is a must-attend for young athletes looking to impress college coaches and enhance their skills.

title: Players Corner ft. Luca Donohue
excerpt: As we continue with our segment, "Players Corner", we have interviewed one of our U12 players and rare pitcher/catcher combo Luca Donohue.
body:
As we continue with our segment, "Players Corner", we have interviewed one of our U12 players and rare pitcher/catcher combo Luca Donohue. He had a promising fall we hope he continues.

1) "Who do you look up to as your heroes in sports?"

Derek Jeter, because of the way he handled himself on and off the field. He treats everyone with respect. He created a great model for leadership as the Yankee captain and I try to follow it. His work with his foundation is inspiring as well as his second baseman.
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: LETTER FROM THE FALCONS
excerpt: As Winter arrives, how will you spend your time preparing for the Spring season? The Winter is here!
body: As Winter arrives, how will you spend your time preparing for the Spring season? The Winter is here! For many of us and our children this means other sports such as basketball or hockey. However for most of us involved in youth travel baseball, Winter is also a time of focused, baseball training.
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: PLAYERS CORNER ft. CARLOS LALANE
excerpt: Carlos Lalane shares who he looks up to as his hero in sports and what his friends think of his athletic success.
body:
1) "Who do you look up to as your heroes in sports?"

I look to Roberto Clemente as my hero in sports.

2) What do your friends think of your athletic success? How do you handle missing out on activities with friends because of your athletic schedule?

My friends find me playing sports kind of cool. But there have been multiple times where they invited me to hang out but I couldn't. I just have to remember that I practice playing to the best of my ability and sacrifices have to be made to do that.

3) What is one thing that you wish people knew about your sport?

One thing I wish people knew about baseball is that it's not just a summer sport. You can play year-round in different leagues and tournaments.

4) How has being part of the Brooklyn Falcons organization impacted your life?

Being part of the Brooklyn Falcons has impacted my life in many ways. It has given me the opportunity to play with some very talented players and coaches that push me to be better every single day. It's also helped me become more disciplined and responsible.

5) What are your future aspirations?

My future aspirations are to play college baseball and hopefully make it to the professional level.
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: A LOOK BACK AT OCTOBER
excerpt: Here is a look back into our Fall teams. Our 14U team spent their fall traveling to North Carolina visiting college campuses such as Duke University and NC State.
body:
Here is a look back into our Fall teams

14U

Our 14U team is not in the showcase baseball mode. They spent their fall traveling to North Carolina where they visited college campuses such as Duke University and NC State. There they received some encouraging words from the head coach of NC state. They also played on the UCONN campus field and worked out with some of the UCONN coaches. They also won a championship this past fall. Our 14U Showcase is now focused on showcasing their skills to college coaches.
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: LETTER FROM THE FALCONS
excerpt: Thank you to all the athletes, parents, families, administrators, trainers, and volunteers that helped make this season special.
body:
Thank you to all the athletes, parents, families, administrators, trainers, and volunteers that helped make this season special. Thank you to the dedicated coaching staff who share their time and enthusiasm for teaching baseball and coaching young men. Congratulations to the athletes who made this year an outstanding, special season. The standard of excellence for the Brooklyn Falcons is at a new height.

While the teams in the program had great win-loss records and they enjoyed their share of big wins, what made this season special was the way in which our young men represented themselves on and off the field. The character, discipline, and work ethic displayed by these athletes set a new standard for the program.
author: Brooklyn Falcons

HERE IS A LOOK BACK INTO MAY

13U COLUMBIA

Our 13U Columbia team has recorded a 13-3-1 tournament record thus far, with a loss in the Perfect Game Championship. They only tie came against a very strong and nationally ranked Body Armour Titans.

13U NAVY

Our Navy team has performed well in league play as well as Perfect Game Tournament that they attended. They went 2-0 beating rivals such as the NY Angels and Los Magos. To reach the playoffs.

12U

Our 12U team has picked up the pace in May. Reaching the playoffs.

title: MAKE A DIFFERENCE
excerpt: Want to help make a difference while you shop in the Amazon app, at no extra cost to you? Simply follow the instructions to select "Bk Falcons" as your charity.
body:
Want to help make a difference while you shop in the Amazon app, at no extra cost to you? Simply follow the instructions below to select "Bk Falcons" as your charity and activate AmazonSmile in the app. They'll donate a portion of your eligible mobile app purchases to us.

How it works:

1. Open the Amazon app on your phone
2. Select the main menu (=) & tap on "AmazonSmile" within Programs & Features
3. Select "Bk Falcons" as your charity
4. Follow the on-screen instructions to activate AmazonSmile in the app.
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: PLAYER'S CORNER
excerpt: Ulysses Pichardo of our U12 Team shares who he looks up to as a hero in sports and what inspires him on and off the field.
body:
Ulysses Pichardo of our U12 Team

1) Who do you look up to as your hero in sports?

I look up to Jackie Robinson as a hero in sports because he broke the color barrier in the MLB and allowed minorities to play baseball. Jackie Robinson was mentally tough because he had to deal with racist fans and political issues. That did not stop him from being a great baseball player.

2) What do your friends think of your athletic success? How do you handle missing out on activities with friends because of your baseball schedule?

My friends think it's cool that I play baseball. I handle missing out on activities with my friends because I know that baseball is very important to me and my future. I believe that the sacrifices I make now will help me later in life.

3) How has baseball impacted your life?

Baseball has impacted my life in many ways. It has taught me discipline, hard work, and dedication. It has also taught me how to be a good teammate and how to work together with others to achieve a common goal.

title: LOOKING BACK
excerpt: Our 13U Columbia team has recorded a 11-2 record thus far, winning the championship and beating the nationally ranked Tri-State Arsenal team 12-7.
body:
HERE IS A LOOK BACK INTO APRIL

13U COLUMBIA
Our 13U Columbia team has recorded a 11-2 record thus far. In their only two tournaments of the year, they have lost in the championship and won the championship beating the nationally ranked Tri-State Arsenal team 12-7. We are looking forward to what we think will be an extraordinary year for this group.

13U NAVY
13U Navy has come together and had a great start to their season. They reached the semi-finals in their last tournament of the month beating another team in pool play.
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: NEW PLAYERS CORNER
excerpt: Getting to know Enzo Landi — who he looks up to as his heroes in sports and how he balances athletics with life.
body:
GETTING TO KNOW ENZO LANDI

1) Who do you look up to as your heroes in sports?
* Jose Altuve
* Derrick Rose
* Mookie Betts
* Michael Jordan

2) What do your friends think of your athletic success? How do you handle missing out on activities with friends because of your athletic schedule?
Its hard at times but my parents really try hard to let me hang out with my friends when not in practice or playing to be with my friends. Most of my friends also play sports so they understand. It's funny when we do get together because we all talk about sports.

title: LETTER FROM THE FALCONS
excerpt: The Brooklyn Falcons are committed to providing professional instruction to dedicated athletes across the socio-economic spectrum.
body:
Opening Season Letter

The Brooklyn Falcons are a youth baseball organization committed to providing professional instruction to dedicated athletes across the socio-economic spectrum. We believe that baseball is more than just a game; it is an opportunity to break racial and economic barriers. Our goal is to build confident, hard-working athletes by putting them in a position to compete, thus gaining confidence through success and learning from failure.

Our mission for the Falcons program is to provide opportunities for youth to develop as baseball players and as people. We strive to create an environment where young athletes can thrive both on and off the field, developing leadership skills, discipline, and character that will serve them throughout their lives.

title: Coach's Corner Interview with Andrew Castano
excerpt: How did you end up becoming a part of our Falcons program? I heard about this wonderful organization from a former teammate of mine, Coach Frank.
body:
COACH'S CORNER INTERVIEW WITH ANDREW CASTANO

How did you end up becoming a part of our Falcons program?

I heard about this wonderful organization from a former teammate of mine, Coach Frank. We go way back to college days and playing summer baseball in West Palm Beach, Florida. I saw him post about tryouts on his Instagram and I messaged him that I was interested in coaching!

Where did you grow up and what sports did you play growing up?

I was originally born in Queens, NY, but I moved to Valley Stream, Long Island when I was about 3 years old. I grew up playing multiple sports. I played football and basketball but my true passion was always baseball. I started playing baseball around the age of 5 and never stopped. Baseball has always been my first love.

What is your coaching philosophy?

My coaching philosophy is to lead by example. I believe that hard work and dedication will get you to where you want to be. I also believe in having fun while competing. Life is too short not to enjoy the game you love. I want my players to leave the field knowing that they gave it their all and had fun doing it.

What are some of your coaching accomplishments?

Some of my accomplishments include coaching multiple all-stars and all-tournament players. I've also had the privilege of coaching players that have gone on to play college baseball. Seeing my players succeed at the next level is one of the greatest accomplishments as a coach.

What do you enjoy most about coaching with the Falcons?

What I enjoy most about coaching with the Falcons is the family atmosphere. Everyone here genuinely cares about each other and wants to see one another succeed. The organization is very supportive and they allow coaches to do what we do best, which is coach and develop players. It's a pleasure being part of such a great organization.
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: Letter from the Falcons
excerpt: We hope everyone is safe and healthy, and you all are enjoying your fall season. There are plenty of new faces on each team and we'd like to extend a warm welcome.
body:
Falcons Family,

We hope everyone is safe and healthy, and you all are enjoying your fall season. There are plenty of new faces on each team and we'd like to extend a warm welcome and hope that you get the most out of the Falcons experience.

Not so long ago, we worried there would be no season, but luckily, we are now winding down on five months of playing before we go into our rest period.

We have had a good fall thus far with most of our teams making it deep into tournament playoffs. It has been great to see the hard work and dedication that our players have put in both on and off the field. We would like to thank all our coaches for their commitment and leadership throughout the season.

As we approach the end of this season, we want to remind everyone of the importance of the Falcons community. Whether you are a player, parent, coach, or supporter, you are part of this family. We encourage everyone to continue supporting one another and celebrating the achievements of all our teams.

Thank you for your continued support of the Brooklyn Falcons organization.
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: Coach's Corner Interview with Michael Gonzalez
excerpt: How did you end up becoming a part of our Falcons program? My good friend Coach Kenny asked me if I was interested in coaching with the Falcons.
body:
How did you end up becoming a part of our Falcons program?
My good friend Coach Kenny asked me if I was interested in coaching with the Falcons.

Where did you grow up and what sports did you play growing up?
I was born in the Dominican Republic and came to Brooklyn when I was four years old and been living in Brooklyn ever since. Growing up I played anything that would keep me outside for a bit longer. If the boys were playing football I'd play football, if they were playing basketball, I'd play basketball, baseball, whatever kept me in the streets.

What position did you play?
I played running back in football and I was a point guard in basketball.

Who was your favorite player growing up?
My favorite player was Michael Jordan. I looked up to him because of his competitiveness and his will to win.

What are some of your coaching philosophies?
My coaching philosophy is to teach these young men to be resilient, to push through adversity, and to understand that hard work pays off. I also want them to understand that being part of a team means being accountable to one another.

What has been your favorite moment coaching with the Falcons?
My favorite moment has been seeing these young men grow not just as athletes but as people. Watching them overcome obstacles and seeing them believe in themselves has been rewarding.

What advice would you give to young athletes?
My advice would be to stay humble, work hard, and never give up on your dreams. Surround yourself with people who believe in you and push you to be better every single day.
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: Falcon Team and Tournament Recaps — October 2020
excerpt: The Fall season for our 10U Falcons led by Coach Ed Jimenez started with very positive results. They are currently playing in a local league with a record of 3-1.
body:
10u

The Fall season for our 10U Falcons led by Coach Ed Jimenez started with very positive results. They are currently playing in a local league and have a record of 3-1. The big plan is developing these young players to reach their potential through coaching and teaching the game the proper way—giving them the tools to become successful and learn what it takes to go from good to great as baseball players.

12u

The 12U Falcons have shown a tremendous amount of improvement, winning 10 out of the last 11 games in their local league. The team has demonstrated solid fundamentals and strong execution on both sides of the ball. Coach Juan Muniz has implemented a strong work ethic and championship mentality with this group. They continue to develop their skills and compete at a high level.

14u

The 14U Falcons competed in several tournaments over the month of October. They displayed excellent pitching and solid defensive play throughout. The team continues to work on consistency at the plate and has made significant strides in team chemistry and communication.

title: Coach's Corner Interview with Franklyn Coste
excerpt: How did you end up becoming a part of our Falcons program? Me and another coach and former teammate, Andrew Medina, were interested in coaching once our playing careers finished.
body:
How did you end up becoming a part of our Falcons program?

Me and another Brooklyn Falcons coach and former teammate, Andrew Medina, were interested in coaching once our playing careers finished, and it just so happened, that one day when Coach Drew was working out, he ran into a Falcons coach. Drew then recommended me, and that is how I got here.

Where did you grow up and what sports did you play growing up?

I grew up in Corona, Queens, and baseball was the only sport I took serious and played.
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: LETTER FROM THE BROOKLYN FALCONS
excerpt: Dear Falcons Family, the season has come to an end. Just a short time ago we thought that there would be no season at all.
body:
Dear Falcons Family,

The Season has come to an end. Just a short time ago we thought that there would be no season at all. With hard work, dedication and caution, we made it through. Our teams gathered a total of five championships and several finalist awards. But as summer winds down, now is when the real work begins.

Fall ball is just around the corner!!! For most it's the end of the season, for champions, it's the start. Fall ball is pivotal to the growth of all baseball players. It is the time when the fundamentals are sharpened and players develop a winning mentality.

We are asking all of our families to embrace this opportunity. Sign your sons up for fall ball. Allow them to compete at the highest level and prepare themselves for the next season.

We are excited about the direction of this organization and look forward to another successful year.

See you soon,

Brooklyn Falcons Organization

title: LETTER FROM CHARLES
excerpt: We hope everyone is staying safe and healthy. We have reached the mid-point of the season and we are proud that all of our teams are getting better from week to week.
body:
Good morning Falcon Family,

We hope everyone is staying safe and healthy. We have reached the mid-point of the season and we are proud that all of our teams are getting better from week to week. Each team has been competitive and our players are learning so much along the way. As we all know our 9U and 11U teams were shut down because of covid-19. Both teams are now back in action. All of our players, parents and staff have tested negative and they have completed their quarantine period. It is important that we continue to follow all the guidelines that have been set by the city and state. We look forward to having these teams compete for the remainder of the season.
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: Coach's Corner Interview with Wendy Gomez
excerpt: I became a Falcon in 2005, my senior year of high school, after playing for the Bonnies with Charles Lalane for several years.
body:
Coach's Corner Interview with Wendy Gomez, head of our 9U Falcons

How did you end up becoming a part of our Falcons program?
I became a Falcon in 2005, my senior year of high school, after playing for the Bonnies with Charles Lalane for several years. So when his brother Carlos Lalane founded the current Falcons program, I came on board.

Where did you grow up and what sports did you play growing up?
I grew up in Bushwick and moved to East New York in my teenage years. I've played organized baseball...
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: Falcons Team and Tournament Report — July 2020
excerpt: The 2023 Falcon season is going well. There's definitely areas of improvement for us, but the good news is that our guys are pulling for each other.
body: 2023 showcase The 2023 Falcon season is going well. There's definitely areas of improvement for us, but the good news is that our guys are pulling for each other. We've had a couple tough games where we've lost on situational defense, and a couple tough games where we lost when the opposing pitching was really good, but overall our season has been really solid. Our guys like each other, and we are measuring up to the competition well. Hopefully, come August, we will take another step forward and finish strong.

title: FALCONS TEAM AND TOURNAMENT REPORT
excerpt: Despite our first rescheduled tournament being cancelled, the 2023 team is really practicing and scrimmaging well.
body:
FALCONS TEAM AND TOURNAMENT REPORT

2023 showcase

Despite our first, rescheduled tournament being cancelled, the 2023 team is really practicing and scrimmaging well. The boys have been through it with the time off, but each evening practice brings us back a little more and a little closer to our potential. We have a lot of baseball coming up in July which we'll keep you posted on! Go Falcons!

2024 showcase

The 2024s kicked off their season with a BSE tournament in Pennsylvania. We shook off some rust early on but came together nicely as the tournament progressed. Great energy and competitiveness amongst the group. Excited to see what the rest of the season has in store!
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: LETTER FROM LUIS
excerpt: It sure felt great to be back out on the field this past weekend. The sounds of gloves popping, bats swinging, and kids cheering brought about a sense of normalcy.
body:
Hello Falcons Family,

It sure felt great to be back out on the field this past weekend. The sounds of gloves popping, bats swinging, and kids cheering, sure brought about a sense of normalcy as we finally begin our season. The excitement and smiles on every face on the field and in the dugout created a sense of relief after months of lockdown. I think it's safe to say that wins and losses really didn't mean much this weekend. It was more of a celebration. Watching our players put on their uniforms and take the field was truly special.
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: Coach's Corner Interview with Sal Taormina
excerpt: Luis was my coach for 16u and 17u. We developed a really strong relationship through those years and when I finally graduated he asked if I wanted to coach.
body:
COACH'S CORNER INTERVIEW WITH SAL TAORMINA

How did you end up becoming a part of our Falcons program?

Luis was my coach for 16u and 17u. We developed a really strong relationship through those years and when I finally graduated he asked if I wanted to coach. And the rest is history…

Where did you grow up and where did you play baseball growing up?

Born and raised in Brooklyn. I played on three teams from seven to eighteen. Our Lady of Grace, Bergen Beach, and Youth Service in that order. YSL from 11 to 18 was where I spent most of my baseball career. We were a very competitive organization and that's where I developed a passion for the game.

What was your best baseball memory?

Winning the 14u AAU National Championship with YSL in 2009. We beat a very tough team from Florida. That was a special group of guys and coaches.

If you could give advice to the young players, what would it be?

Work hard, stay humble, and always be a good teammate. Baseball is a team sport and individual stats don't matter if your team isn't winning. Also, enjoy the process and have fun playing the game you love.

What do you enjoy most about coaching?

Seeing kids develop not just as players but as people. Teaching them life lessons through baseball. Building relationships with families in the organization. And of course, winning championships!
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: Coach's Corner Interview with Kenny Duverge
excerpt: Before I became a Falcon, I was an assistant coach for the Brooklyn Bulldogs with Coach Charles working with their 12u Fall team.
body:
How did you end up becoming a part of our Falcons program?

Before I became a Falcon, I was an assistant coach for the Brooklyn Bulldogs with Coach Charles working with their 12u Fall team. Charles approached me after my High School season was over and asked if I wanted to coach again the following Fall and I didn't hesitate. Things clicked with Coach Charles and me right away and I ended up joining him again in the summer but this time with the Bulldogs 11u travel team. During that time in the summer, we started building the Falcons program and Charles asked me to be a part of it and I said yes immediately.

excerpt: Balls, bats, bases and more — we have a charity wish list posted at Amazon. This is a super easy way to donate.
body: Balls, bats, bases and more...we have a charity wish list posted at Amazon. This is a super easy way to donate, just follow the link below. Also, if you shop on Amazon (and we know you do), sign in and assign "Bk Falcons" as your designated charity and a small percentage of your purchases will come our way. Thank you! (Now go do it...)
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: Letter from Luis and Charles
excerpt: Our primary focus is on the safety and well-being of all of our members. We will continue to monitor the progression of COVID-19.
body:
Dear Falcons Family,

We understand that many parents and players want to get back to the field, and so do we, but our primary focus is on the safety and well-being of all of our members. The Brooklyn Falcons will continue to monitor the progression of the Coronavirus Disease (COVID-19) to determine when it is safe to return to activities after May 15. Just because we can't participate in outdoor activities, doesn't mean we can't get better. We have uploaded the drill of the week to the blog on our website for all of our players to work on while we are apart.

Our organization is committed to supporting our community during these challenging times. We look forward to reuniting with our Falcons Family soon and continuing our mission to develop young athletes both on and off the field.
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: Coach's Corner Interview with Carlos Lalane
excerpt: Managed the Men's Brooklyn Falcons Baseball team from 2004 to 2011 and from 2013 to 2016. Then coached 8U through 10U Falcons.
body: A little background on Carlos…Managed the Men's Brooklyn Falcons Baseball team from 2004 to 2011 and from 2013 to 2016. Then coached 8U through 10U Falcons. Attended Canarsie HS where I played baseball, football and volleyball. I stopped playing high school baseball my junior year, let's say I lost interest. Started playing football and volleyball. I was offered an opportunity to play college football at a Division III school but I chose to keep my bones intact and I accepted an academic scholarship.
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: Drill of the Week
excerpt: Hope everyone is healthy and safe. As we all know, our season has been on hold due to the Covid-19 situation.
body:
Good afternoon Falcon Family,

Hope everyone is healthy and safe. As we all know, our season has been on hold due to the Covid-19 situation. But that doesn't mean that we can't try to stay as ready as possible. Every week we will release a drill of the week. This is to help us try to stay as ready as possible for the baseball season. Stay safe and stay healthy.

title: Letter From Luis and Charles
excerpt: One of the most important attributes of an athlete is their response to adversity.
body:
Hello Falcons Family,

One of the most important attributes of an athlete is their response to adversity. Can a pitcher stay composed with the tying run on second and no-one out? Are you in a relaxed state of mind when the go-ahead run is on third and you're up at-bat? Are you confident enough to give your body for your team in an attempt to make a diving catch in a big moment? Now we as athletes, coaches and parents are faced with a different kind of challenge. One that has no face and will not disappear overnight.
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: Coach's Corner
excerpt: What have you been doing to keep busy these past few weeks? Creating lessons for remote learning and brushing up on Madden, NBA 2K and MLB 2020 skills.
body:
What have you been doing to keep busy these past few weeks?

I have been creating lessons for my new journey (remote learning) and brushing up on my Madden, NBA 2K and MLB 2020 skills so I can challenge any and all of our Falcons players, and of course win. Any takers?

Tell us about your favorite moment playing or coaching baseball.

After playing high school baseball at a top level and facing future hall of famers in college, I believed for a while that I have failed at baseball because I didn't make it to the major leagues. It wasn't until I started coaching that I realized my purpose wasn't to play professionally, but to positively impact the lives of young athletes and help them achieve their dreams. That realization changed my perspective on success and failure.
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: Falcons Players Talk Baseball with ex-Big League Pitcher Pedro Beato
excerpt: Three Falcons 12U players sat down with former Mets starter Pedro Beato this past Martin Luther King Day to find out what it's like to play in the Big Leagues.
body:
What's it like to play in the Big Leagues? Three Falcons 12U players sat down with former Mets starter Pedro Beato this past Martin Luther King Day to find out.

Beato, a 6'6" Dominican pitcher who spent his childhood in Ridgewood, Queens, played for Youth Services League, where he got to know Falcon's leadership Luis Monell and Charles Lalane.

After his debut with the Mets, Beato played with the Red Sox, Braves and Phillies. Currently a free agent, the big righty racked up 60 strikeouts in four weeks during his professional career.
author: Brooklyn Falcons

title: Great Turnout at Tryouts!

body: Thank you and good luck to the 90+ballplayers who came to tryouts this weekend for our 10U-16U teams. We feel honored and blessed to have so many players interested in our program in its first official year. A special thanks to New York Premier Baseball Performance Center for their support and for making Premier the official home of the Falcons. 2019 is going to be a special year!!!
